title: grewia sapida
intro: Grewia sapida, a plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae, is commonly known as the Indian plum or Phalsa. The plant is native to the Indian subcontinent and is widely used in traditional medicine, valued for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Grewia sapida include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Grewia sapida is often prepared as a decoction or a powder to be used in herbal remedies, and it is also consumed as a fruit to reap its nutritional benefits.
